scope: Unified parameter-to-capability index across explicit API parameters, auxiliary or candidate parameters, and replay-closure tooling surfaces.
purpose: Give one machine-readable entrypoint from parameter to capability to recipe to regression sample to evidence.
Reduce the need to jump manually between the parameter inventory, closure matrix, capability recipes, and replay-tooling notes.
interpretation_notes: Empty related_regression_samples is expected for tooling-only entries and for candidate-parameter families that currently have no dedicated regression-case row in regression_samples_20260620.json.

Empty related_recipes does not automatically mean the parameter is undocumented; some candidate-parameter families currently only map to evidence artifacts and remaining-gap tracking, not to a dedicated invocation recipe.
This index is curated for navigation and traceability. It is not the authoritative source for branch semantics; when the index and a deeper artifact disagree, prefer the deeper source artifact.

entries.role: request routing or config selector; 431 is the canonical positive cover branch, 453 is a noncanonical cover-only positive shell, 476 is a caller-facing imgtag-family ultra-thin shell, 483 is a video_ids-led thin shell, 506 is now a caller-facing empty-valued field shell, and 537 remains a thinner success-shell family rather than a caller-default replacement
remaining_gaps: Whether API1 has any positive or success-shell tid beyond the current 431/453/476/483/506/537 family map is still open.
Whether the mapped noncanonical API1 shell families change thickness under aged-cookie or login-state replay remains open.

entries.role: comma-separated CID list with tested 32 non-empty item success boundary and 33 item failure boundary
remaining_gaps: Aged-cookie/login-state replay is still open for this row, but repeated same-name idlist keys already behave as first-value-wins and do not merge across query positions in the tested same-day anonymous plus real anonymous visitor-cookie replay collision cases.

entries.role: numeric canonical appid enables the stricter documented branch; missing or text-like values currently hit a looser anonymous-compatible branch in tested cases, and the new numeric-focus, contiguous-band, plus boundary-search slices show a third tested family near the canonical value that falls into appkey-gated rejection rather than appid-no-find
remaining_gaps: Full numeric appid space is still not closed; the new evidence now narrows it to a tested three-family split with explicit cutovers `10001000 -> 10001001` and `10001186 -> 10001187`, but not to a global numeric-space proof outside the searched regions.

entries.role: validated when the canonical numeric appid branch is active; the new numeric-focus slice shows that tested neighboring numeric appids such as `10001004` stay on `10010110 / appkey error` whether appkey is canonical, missing, or explicitly wrong, the contiguous-band scan extends that tested rejection family across `10001001-10001004` and `10001006-10001020`, and the boundary search currently carries it up to `10001186` before flipping back
remaining_gaps: No evidence yet that appkey unlocks additional API1 capability beyond satisfying the exact canonical numeric-appid branch; the tested near-canonical numeric family still rejects the current canonical appkey across the searched `10001001-10001186` region.

entries.role: response wrapper selector; only exact lowercase json switches the tested branch into JSONP
remaining_gaps: No positive wrapper value has been confirmed beyond XML default and lowercase JSONP.

entries.role: request routing or config selector; 535 is the canonical full-detail branch, 488/540/541 are thinner alternate positive shells, 502 is the richer alternate positive shell, and 506 is currently a success-shell family rather than a detail recipe
remaining_gaps: Whether API2 has any positive or success-shell tid outside the current 488/502/506/535/540/541 family map is still open, but the lower-edge 522-529 band and the mid-corridor 456-521 follow-up are now materially tighter than the older 535/540/541-only reading.
Dedicated caller-facing validation now confirms tid=506 as a near-empty success shell in the anonymous direct-call scope; broader samples and aged-cookie/login-state replay remain open.

entries.role: the `20001238 + 6c03bbe9658448a4` pair is the current canonical API2 branch gate; missing or text-like appid currently bypasses that stricter branch in tested cases, and the new numeric-focus, contiguous-band, plus boundary-search slices show a third tested family near the canonical value that falls into appkey-gated rejection rather than appid-no-find
remaining_gaps: Full numeric appid space is still not closed; the new evidence now narrows it to a tested three-family split with explicit cutovers `20001000 -> 20001001` and `20002581 -> 20002582`, but not to a global numeric-space proof outside the searched regions.

entries.role: the appkey side of the canonical `20001238 + 6c03bbe9658448a4` pair is only validated when that exact numeric-appid branch is active; the new numeric-focus slice shows that tested neighboring numeric appids such as `20001237` stay on `10010110 / appkey error` whether appkey is canonical, missing, or explicitly wrong, the contiguous-band scan extends that tested rejection family across `20001220-20001237` and `20001239-20001250`, and the boundary search currently carries it across the wider searched `20001001-20002581` region before flipping back
remaining_gaps: No evidence yet that appkey unlocks additional API2 capability beyond satisfying the exact canonical numeric-appid branch; the tested near-canonical numeric family still rejects the current canonical appkey across the searched `20001001-20002581` region.

entries.role: present in canonical examples but currently no visible branch effect has been proven in anonymous canonical runs, real anonymous visitor-cookie replay, or the focused 535/540/541 x union_platform capability sweep
unlocked_capabilities: no distinct capability unlocked in current tested environments
remaining_gaps: Whether union_platform matters under aged-cookie or login-state replay remains open.
Whether union_platform matters on broader public vids outside the current 535/540/541 anchor sweep remains open.

entries.role: comma-separated VID list with tested 32 non-empty item success boundary and 33 item failure boundary
remaining_gaps: Aged-cookie/login-state replay is still open for this row, but repeated same-name idlist keys already behave as first-value-wins and do not merge across query positions in the tested same-day anonymous plus real anonymous visitor-cookie replay collision cases.

entries.role: rewrites JSONP wrapper prefix in the tested JSONP branch; current practical value-space includes both raw-passthrough and parse-breaking families, with `[` / `[[` still on the raw-passthrough side while `a(` / `((` / `})();` plus `[(` / `{(` / `/*(` / `a[(` / `)(` / `](` / `}(` sit in the current parse-breaking representative family. Repeated callback now behaves as first-value-wins in the tested same-day anonymous and real anonymous visitor-cookie replay collision cases, and tested wrong-appkey JSONP error shells still honor callback wrapper control.
remaining_gaps: Broader parser-breaking families remain open beyond the newly widened unmatched-delimiter / odd-prefix representatives.
Aged-cookie and login-state replay are still needed before callback closure can be promoted beyond anonymous real-cookie scope.

entries.role: candidate auth or session-coupled query-key family including token, sign, sig, appver, access_token, authkey, and openid
remaining_gaps: Aged-cookie replay under the same visitor flow.
Real login-state replay.
Whether any auth-leaning key becomes meaningful only under aged-cookie or login-state environments.

entries.role: candidate format or version or platform or source-like query-key family including format, output, version, v, platform, and source
remaining_gaps: Whether a narrower reserved-looking key family exists outside the currently tested set.
Whether any reserved-looking key changes behavior only under aged-cookie or login-state environments.

entries.role: convert raw desktop or mobile Cookie headers into replay env JSON for real, aged, or login-state runs
remaining_gaps: Still needs real aged-cookie and login-state inputs before the resulting parameter conclusions can be upgraded.

entries.role: run the canonical contract matrix across replay header environments
remaining_gaps: Still needs real aged-cookie and login-state inputs before the resulting parameter conclusions can be upgraded.

entries.role: replay auth-ish and callback or repeated-key semantics under a chosen env; currently also supports dynamic callback-tail values via extra-callback forwarding
remaining_gaps: Still needs real aged-cookie and login-state inputs before the resulting parameter conclusions can be upgraded.

entries.role: one-command orchestration for env-json build, matrix replay, and semantics replay; currently supports full semantics, callback-tail forwarding, and writable artifact or staging directories
remaining_gaps: Still needs real aged-cookie and login-state inputs before the resulting parameter conclusions can be upgraded.

current_limitations: This index does not prove aged-cookie or login-state closure by itself; it only links the current evidence graph.
Replay tooling entries describe how to run the closure workflow, not that the aged-cookie or login-state conclusions are already achieved.
